Overview Capabilities Sectors Served Execution Lifecycle Software & Tech Featured Projects FAQs
01 / OVERVIEW

Vertical Overview

Our Construction Supervision and Management team ensures that designs are executed accurately, safely, and on schedule in the field. We deploy construction managers, site engineers, piping supervisors, structural steel experts, and HSE auditors to manage site activities. We coordinate contractor interfaces, resolve constructability issues, oversee heavy lifts, and enforce strict safety policies, keeping construction sites hazard-free.

Business Value Delivery

We maintain a Zero-LTI (Lost Time Injury) record across our managed sites. Our constructability reviews and contractor interface coordination minimize field clashes, preventing expensive delays and keeping projects on budget.

Key Differentiators

  • Strict field safety auditing systems ensuring OSHA and local HSE compliance.
  • Real-time progress tracking linked to Primavera P6 construction curves.
  • Integrated clash resolution matching site conditions back to 3D designs.
02 / CAPABILITIES

Specialized Workflows

Construction Interface Management

We coordinate boundaries between civil, structural, piping, and electrical contractors, preventing work stoppages and conflicts.

Rigging & Heavy Lift Supervision

Our rigging engineers review lift plans, verify crane capacities, and supervise the erection of massive vessels and equipment blocks safely.

Site Quality Assurance & Welding Inspections

We perform weld mapping, coordinate NDT checks (X-ray, UT), inspect concrete pours, and compile site quality records.

03 / SECTORS SERVED

Industries Supported

Industrial Facilities
Substation Erection
Refinery Refurbishment
Modular Yard Operations
Infrastructure Construction
04 / EXECUTION TIMELINE

Project Delivery Stages

Interactive tracking of progress gates from bid to delivery handovers.

Stage 01 / Constructability Review & Planning

We review engineering drawings for field constructability and draft site execution plans.

Stage 02 / Civil Foundations & Groundwork

Supervising piling, concrete foundation pours, and anchor bolt placements.

Stage 03 / Steel & Mechanical Erection

Supervising pipe racks, structural framing, equipment placement, and piping runs.

Stage 04 / Quality Sign-Off & Walkdowns

We conduct punch-list walkdowns, verify weld records, and sign off blocks.

05 / TECH ECOSYSTEM

Software Stack & Toolsets

Primavera P6 EPPM

Schedule Supervision

Navisworks Manage

Constructability Review

BIM 360

Field Quality Management

Aconex Field

Defect Tracking & Site Punching

Excel Site Trackers

Welding Progress Logs
06 / CASE STUDY

Execution in Practice

Refinery De-bottlenecking Construction

The Challenge

An active chemical plant required the installation of a new compressor unit and pipe bridge with zero impact to ongoing operations.

Our Approach

We drafted detailed construction plans, staging heavy steel framing components in an offsite yard. We coordinated crane placements and supervised the critical lifts during a scheduled 36-hour shutdown window.

The Result

The steel and compressor were erected, bolted, and inspected in 30 hours, returning the plant to operations 6 hours ahead of the shutdown limit.

07 / EXECUTED PROJECTS

Related Experience

Pipe Rack Structural Erection Supervision

Mangalore, India

Supervision of 1,200 tons of structural steel pipe rack erection, heavy equipment placement, and pipe routing.

24,500 Hours Completed

Power Plant Turbine Erection Oversight

Nagpur, India

Construction supervision, mechanical alignment checks, and lifting plans review for a 60MW steam turbine generator.

16,800 Hours Completed

Tank Farm Piping Construction Supervision

Haldia, India

Field supervision of carbon steel and stainless steel piping runs, tank hookups, and field welding inspections.

14,300 Hours Completed
08 / QUESTIONS & ANSWERS

Frequently Asked Questions

How do you manage site safety (HSE)?

We enforce absolute compliance with site safety policies, including daily toolbox talks, permit-to-work audits, and PPE checks.

How are field changes handled?

We issue Site Clarification Requests (SCRs) or Field Change Notices (FCNs), coordinating with designers to resolve discrepancies.

Do you supply heavy construction equipment?

No, we act as construction managers, reviewing crane plans and logistics, while contractors supply the physical equipment.

What is your process for welding quality inspections?

We verify welder certifications, monitor weld preheating, map welds in a database, and coordinate NDT inspections.
